payload.name: capture
payload.description: Capture a task onto the board. Join argv into one task text and emit task.captured; new tasks start in the Now lane. The board's capture box and the brain both call this.

params.text: string — the task, joined from argv

payload.implementation:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# capture — drop anything onto the board. Joins argv into one task and emits a
# single task.captured event; new tasks start in the "now" lane. Pipe contract:
# args via argv, log as JSONL on stdin (unused), new events as JSONL on stdout.
import sys, json
text = " ".join(sys.argv[1:]).strip()
if not text:
    text = "(empty task)"
print(json.dumps({"name": "task.captured", "payload": {"text": text}}))

payload.name: move
payload.description: Move a task to a lane. Positional argv: task id (the seq of its task.captured event) then lane (now | this_week | waiting | done; friendly spellings normalized). Emits task.moved. The board's per-card buttons and the brain both call this.
payload.implementation:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# move — send a task to a lane. argv is positional: the task's id (the seq of its
# task.captured event) then the lane (now | this_week | waiting | done). Friendly
# spellings are normalized. Emits one task.moved event.
import sys, json
args = sys.argv[1:]
task = args[0] if args else ""
lane = " ".join(args[1:]).strip().lower() or "now"
ALIAS = {"this week": "this_week", "thisweek": "this_week", "todo": "now",
         "inbox": "now", "today": "now", "complete": "done", "completed": "done",
         "finished": "done", "blocked": "waiting", "later": "waiting"}
lane = ALIAS.get(lane, lane)
if lane not in ("now", "this_week", "waiting", "done"):
    lane = "now"
try:
    task = int(task)
except (ValueError, TypeError):
    pass
print(json.dumps({"name": "task.moved", "payload": {"task": task, "lane": lane}}))

payload.name: board
payload.description: A Now / This week / Waiting / Done board. Folds task.captured (task id = its seq, starts in Now) and task.moved (id, lane) into lanes, rendering each task as a card with one-click move forms (posting to /run/move) and a capture box at top (posting to /run/capture). Bare semantic HTML, kernel class vocabulary.

examples.note: shows the lanes and places a moved task under its lane
